2605.17829.title: Interactive Evaluation Requires a Design Science
2605.17829.paper_summary: AI evaluation is undergoing a structural change. Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as systems that act over time through tools, environments, users, and other agents, while many evaluation practices still inherit assumptions from response-centered benchmarks (e.g., fixed inputs, isolated outputs, and outcome judgments that can be made from a single response). The field has begun to build interactive benchmarks, but the resulting landscape is fragmented: benchmarks differ in what interaction artifacts they admit, how trajectories are scored, and what claims their results support. This position paper argues that interactive evaluation should be treated as a principled evaluation paradigm, not merely a new family of agent benchmarks. Simply adopting previous evaluation paradigms does not suffice. We define evaluation as an autonomous mapping from evidence to judgments, and show that interactive evaluation changes both sides of this mapping: the evidence becomes interaction-generated trajectories, while the evaluation procedure must assess process, recoverability, coordination, robustness, and system-level performance. Building on this definition, we propose a two-axis taxonomy, derive design principles and reporting standards, examine representative scenarios, and analyze how longstanding evaluation challenges reappear at the trajectory level.
2605.17829.paper_summary_zh: AI 評估正在經歷結構性的變化。大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被部署為隨著時間推移通過工具、環境、用戶和其他代理進行操作的系統，而許多評估實踐仍然繼承了以反應為中心的基準的假設（例如，固定的輸入、孤立的輸出和可以從單一反應中得出的結果判斷）。該領域已經開始建立互動基準，但結果的格局是支離破碎的：基準在接受哪些互動產物、如何評分軌跡以及其結果支持哪些主張方面各不相同。這篇立場論文主張，互動評估應被視為一種原則性的評估範式，而不僅僅是一種新的代理基準系列。僅僅採用先前的評估範式是不夠的。我們將評估定義為從證據到判斷的自主映射，並展示互動評估如何改變這一映射的雙方：證據變成了互動生成的軌跡，而評估程序必須評估過程、可恢復性、協調性、穩健性和系統級性能。基於這一定義，我們提出了一個雙軸分類法，推導設計原則和報告標準，檢查代表性場景，並分析長期存在的評估挑戰如何在軌跡層面重新出現。
2605.17829.author: Keyang Xuan et.al.
2605.17829.authors: Keyang Xuan, Peiyang Song, Pan Lu, Pengrui Han, Wenkai Li, Zhenyu Zhang, Zexue He, Wenyue Hua, Manling Li, Jiaxuan You, Adrian Weller, Yizhong Wang, Jiaxin Pei
